A sample of radioactive material initially has an activity of 106 decays/min. After 7 d 6 h, the activity is measured to be 66 decays/min. Find the half-life of this material.

An experiment was done with five blocks, made of the same material, in which a horizontal force was applied that pulled each block across a table at constant speed. The data was plotted and the LSRL was sketched. Use the graph above to find the coefficient of sliding friction between the table and the material the blocks are made of.
A 120 $\mu$F capacitor is charged to 24.0 V and then it is connected to a 48.0 $\mu$H inductor. Find the frequency of oscillation.
The __________ telescope is located on Mount Fowlkes, in the Davis Mountains of Texas. This large telescope has a 10-meter aperture and became operational in the 1990s.
The number 0.00045670 has __________ significant digits.

Joe stands on a platform 2.66 m above the ground and throws a ball with a velocity of 22.4 m/s at an angle of $36.8^{\circ}$ above the horizontal. How long is the ball in the air before it hits the ground?

A 12.0-kg block is placed on top of a 20.0-kg block, which lies on a frictionless, horizontal surface. When a horizontal force of 72.0 N is applied to the 12-kg block, it accelerates at $3.21~m/s^{2}$. Find the acceleration of the 20-kg block while the 12-kg block is in contact.

Tim, a 36.0-kg boy, starts from rest at the top of the slide as shown above. The coefficient of friction between Tim's pants and the surface of the slide is 0.193. How fast is Tim traveling just as he reaches the bottom?

After Tim leaves the slide, he heads toward a merry-go-round with a 2.25-m radius and moment of inertia of $625~kg\cdot m^{2}$ that is at rest. Tim runs along a path that is tangential to the rim of the merry-go-round at a speed of $5.00~m/s$ and jumps on. It begins to rotate about a frictionless pivot. What is the maximum kinetic energy obtained by the boy-merry-go-round system?

At a Kermit home game, Mrs. Martinez was yelling encouragement to the Yellowjacket players at a sound level of 96.0 dB. The cheerleaders jumped up and got the crowd excited. If 99 people sitting very close to Mrs. Martinez began yelling at the same sound level as her, what is the combined sound level of all 100 people?
Two moles of an ideal monotonic gas are heated at constant volume from $30.0^{\circ}C$ to $300.0^{\circ}C$. Find the heat added to the gas.
Find the current flowing through the 48 Ω resistor.
A charge of 60.0 µC is placed at $x=-3.00$ cm on the x-axis and a second charge of 90.0 µC is placed at the origin. Find the magnitude of the electric field at a point on the y-axis at $y=-4.00$ cm.
A solenoid is 44.0 cm long, has a radius of 1.04 cm, has 1200 turns and carries a current of 9.00 A. Find the magnetic field at the center of the solenoid.

A uniform magnetic field is perpendicular to the plane of a loop with a radius of 7.55 cm. The resistance of the loop is 1.22 Ω. The magnetic field is increasing at a rate of 825 mT/s. Find the magnitude of the induced current in the loop.
Ted has a 2.00 in-tall candle and a convex mirror with a radius of curvature of 24.0 cm. How far from the mirror should Ted place the candle to produce a virtual image that is 0.800 in tall?
According to the Bohr model, what is the wavelength of light emitted when a hydrogen atom makes the transition from the $n=6$ level to the $n=3$ level?
The Delta minus particle has a quark content of ddd. Which is true about this particle? $B=$ Baryon number $Q=$ Charge $S=$ Strangeness number $C=$ Charm number

An experiment was done in which five different blocks were hung from the end of a horizontal uniform beam and the tension in the cord was measured. The beam is free to pivot about a pin attached to the wall. The cord will snap when the tension exceeds 100 N. Find the maximum weight that can be hung from the end of the beam.
A coil and a resistor are placed across the terminals of a 12 V battery. What is the current after 200 µs? The battery has negligible internal resistance.

NASA, the ESA and the ASI collaborated to send a probe to study Saturn, the rings of Saturn and the moons of Saturn. The Cassini-Huygens space-research mission involved the use of the Cassini probe provided by NASA and the Huygens lander provided by the ESA. The Cassini probe entered the orbit of Saturn while the Huygens lander successfully landed on Saturn's largest moon.
If one parsec equals 3.26 light years and the distance from Earth to Proxima Centauri is 1.3 parsecs, then the distance from Earth to Proxima Centauri is __________ kilometers.
What is the range of a cannon if the cannonballs leave the barrel of the cannon at a speed of $333 \text{ m/s}$ and at an angle of 45.0° above the horizontal? Ignore air resistance.

Jan can drive her 1000-kg car at a maximum speed of $108 \text{ km/h}$ without skidding on a curved section of a highway near Andrews. If the radius of the curve is 125 m and the curve is unbanked, find the coefficient of friction between the road and the tires of her car. ($108 \text{ km/h} = 67.1 \text{ mph}$ and the posted speed limit on the curve is 55 mph)

A 1.66-kg block is released from rest at a height of 3.55 m above a vertical spring. The spring is compressed 12.2 cm when the block is momentarily at rest. Find the speed of the block when the spring is compressed 6.10 cm.

In high school, Jimmy owned a Pontiac GTO with a tri-powered 389 cubic inch V-8 engine that delivered 360 horsepower at 5200 rpm. Find the torque delivered by this engine. ($1 \text{ hp} = 746 \text{ W}$)

Sound travels at a speed of $1470 \text{ m/s}$ through the water in Lake Texoma and slows to a speed of 341 m/s when it travels in the air above the water. If sound with a frequency of 512 Hz is made under the water, what is the frequency of the sound when it is traveling through the air?

Water flows through a section of horizontal pipe at 3.12 L/s. The pressure in this section of pipe is 148 kPa and the diameter is 2.24 cm. The pressure at a certain point is to be reduced to 98.0 kPa by constricting a section of the pipe. Find the diameter of the constricted section of pipe.
If the current flowing through the ammeter is 0.1542 A, then $R$ = __________ $\Omega$.
A metal sphere with a radius of 3.88 cm is at a potential of 240 V. How many electrons have been removed from the sphere to raise it to this potential?

An alpha particle, $m = 6.65 \times 10^{-27} \text{ kg}$, is traveling in a circular path with a radius of 25.0 cm. The plane of the circular path is perpendicular to a 0.855-T magnetic field. Find the kinetic energy of the alpha particle.

The conducting rod above is 24.0 cm long and it is moving at $6.78 \text{ m/s}$ to the right across the page. A 0.523 T magnetic field is directed into the page. Find the potential difference between the ends of the rod.

Martha has a 2.00 in-tall candle and a diverging lens with an unknown focal length. When she places the candle 12.0 cm from the lens, an erect image is formed that is 1.50 in tall. What is the magnitude of the focal length of the lens?
Light with a wavelength of 240 nm is incident on a metal target. The velocity of the most energetic electrons emitted is $295,000 \text{ m/s}$. Find the threshold frequency of the metal target.
The Xi particle $\Xi^-$ has a quark content of dss. Which is true about this particle? $B = \text{Baryon number}$, $Q = \text{Charge}$, $S = \text{Strangeness number}$, $C = \text{Charm number}$

An experiment was done with a conical pendulum in order to find the acceleration of gravity on the surface of Pluto. The angle the string made with the vertical was 7.50° on each trial, but the length was changed on each trial. The data was plotted and a LSRL was calculated. Use the graph above to find the value obtained.

A parallel plate capacitor has square plates with each side 20.0 cm long and with a plate separation of 3.00 mm. The plates are charged to a potential of 36.0 V and the battery is then disconnected. A dielectric slab, $k = 5$, with the same area as the plates, but with a thickness of 1.00 mm is inserted between the plates as shown above. The distance from each plate to the slab is 1.00 mm. Find the capacitance after the dielectric is in place.

Galaxies are remarkable objects, each shining with the light of hundreds of billions of stars. But galaxies are not as luminous as the most brilliant beacons of all: __________ They “are phenomenally powerful, pouring forth the luminosity of a trillion to a thousand trillion Suns.”
The speed of light is $3 \times 10^8 \, \text{m/s}$ which is equal to __________ $km/day$.

Coach Allen wants the Highland Park punter to have a "hang time" of 4.82 s and he wants the ball to hit the ground 50.8 m from the punter. If the ball leaves the punter's foot 1.42 m above the ground, find the magnitude of the velocity of the football just as it leaves the punter's foot.
Ted ($m = 67.3 \, \text{kg}$) is standing on a platform scale in an elevator. What does the scale read while the elevator is accelerating upward at $1.55 \, \text{m/s}^2$?

A dart with a mass of 9.25 g is fired vertically upward from a spring gun. The spring must be compressed 7.52 cm in order for the dart to reach a height of 22.4 m above the dart's position on the compressed spring. Find the spring constant of the spring.

A horizontal disk with a mass of 150 g and a radius of 36.0 cm was rotating freely about a vertical axis through its center at 48.0 rpm. A 25.6 g piece of chewing gum is dropped from above and sticks to the edge of the disk. Find the linear speed of the piece of chewing gum. ($I_{\text{disk}} = \frac{1}{2}mr^2$)
Consider a 24.0-cm-long string fixed at both ends. It has a mass of 1.22 g and it is vibrating at a fundamental frequency of 880 Hz. Find the tension in the string.
The __________ law of thermodynamics states that if two systems are in thermal equilibrium with a third system, then they are in thermal equilibrium with each other.
The capacitor was initially uncharged. If the switch was closed at $t = 0$, then the potential difference across the resistor at $t = 0.00576 \, \text{s}$ is __________ V.

Consider a spherical drop of water carrying a charge of 60 pC and with a potential of 600 V at its surface. Three other drops with the same radius and same charge combine with the first drop to form one large spherical drop. Find the potential at the surface of this large drop.

A straight, horizontal length of copper wire is 4.56 m long and has a mass of 200 g. A current of 56.2 A flows through the wire. What is the magnitude of the minimum magnetic field needed to balance the force of gravity and suspend the wire?

A 60.0 cm length of copper wire has a radius of 0.754 mm. It is formed into a circular loop and is placed perpendicular to a uniform magnetic field that is increasing at a rate of 12.2 mT/s. At what rate is thermal energy generated in the loop? ($\rho_{\text{copper}} = 1.69 \times 10^{-8} \, \Omega \cdot \text{m}$)

Arlene has very good eyes. Her eyes can see visible light with wavelengths ranging from 380 nm to 750 nm. If she has a diffraction grating with 400 lines per mm, how many orders of her entire visible spectrum can it produce in addition to the $m = 0$ order?
Light with a wavelength of 520 nm is incident on a niobium surface. The speed of the fastest electrons ejected from the surface is 174,000 m/s. What is the work function of niobium?
The Standard Model includes twelve elementary particles known as fermions. These are divided into two groups - quarks and leptons. There are __________ quarks and __________ leptons.

The physics class at Sabine did an experiment to find the moment of inertia of an object they described as a hollow sphere. They measured the mass of the object to be 2.00 kg and the radius to be 40.0 cm. They released the object at the top of an inclined plane and it rolled to the bottom of the incline without slipping. They measured the height of the top of the incline and the linear speed of the object just as it reached the bottom of the incline for five trials. They plotted the data and calculated the LSRL as shown above. Find the value the Sabine students obtained for the moment of inertia of the object.

The physics class at Ponder did an experiment with three capacitors, a resistor and a battery. The respective capacitances were $24 \, \mu\text{F}$, $36 \, \mu\text{F}$ and $48 \, \mu\text{F}$. If they arranged the capacitors in parallel, what was the net capacitance of the arrangement?
The aperture of a telescope is 10.0 cm. If the aperture is increased to 25.0 cm, the light-gathering power of the telescope will increase by a factor of __________.
Convert $64.5$ miles/hour to meters/second.

Joe stands at the edge of the Canadian River bridge and drops a rock. If the rock fell 64.5 m before striking the water below, how far did the rock travel during its final second in the air? Ignore air resistance.

Mary is in a jet plane accelerating down the runway. She holds the top end of a string that has a golf ball attached to the bottom end. The string makes an angle of $21.2^{\circ}$ with the vertical. Find the acceleration of the plane.

A 1700-kg Ford Mustang traveling west at 57.6 km/h collides at an intersection with a 1143-kg Honda Civic that is traveling south at 43.2 km/h. The cars stick together. Find the speed of the wreckage just after the collision.

A 22.0-kg child on a swing is being pulled back by her mother. The mother exerts a force of 110.0 N at an angle of $15.0^{\circ}$ above the horizontal. Find the tension in each of the two ropes supporting the swing. The ropes are vertical.
A physical pendulum consists of a uniform 1.44-kg rod that is 1.22 m long. The rod is pivoted about a point that is 0.225 m from one end. Find the period of the pendulum for small oscillations.

A 1.66-kg block is released from rest at the top of a $33.3^{\circ}$ incline. If the coefficient of kinetic friction between the block and the incline is 0.246, find the speed of the block after it has traveled 2.45 m down the incline.
Find the equivalent resistance between point A and point B for the circuit shown below.

A point charge of $+180.0 \, \mu\text{C}$ is placed at A, a point charge of $+120 \, \mu\text{C}$ is placed at B, and a point charge of $-150 \, \mu\text{C}$ is placed at C. Find the magnitude of the electric force exerted on the point charge at C.
A vertical wire carries a current of 1.22 A straight up. What is the direction of the magnetic field due to the current-carrying wire at a point 1.22 m east of the wire?

A 200-turn circular coil has a radius of 2.22 cm and a resistance of $10.0 \, \Omega$. The plane of the coil is perpendicular to a uniform 1.68-T magnetic field. If the direction of the field is reversed in 0.0259 s, find the average current in the coil.
A diverging lens has a focal length of magnitude 18.0 cm. An object is placed 12.0 cm from the lens. Find the magnification of the image.
The omega minus particle ($\Omega^-$) has a strangeness of $-3$. What is the quark composition of the $\Omega^-$ particle?
Find the threshold frequency for a calcium surface. The work function for calcium is 2.87 eV.
The drag force exerted on an 80.0 kg skydiver is proportional to the square of the velocity. Use the graph above to find the terminal velocity of the skydiver.

Two slits separated by $7.75 \, \mu\text{m}$ are illuminated by monochromatic red light with a wavelength of 660 nm. Find the distance from the first bright fringe to the third bright fringe on a screen 2.00 m away.
One of the most spectacular surprises in Solar System exploration was the discovery of active volcanoes on __________, the innermost of the four large moons of Jupiter.
Which of the following is not an SI base unit?
A ball is launched from the edge of the roof of a building at an angle of $42.2^{\circ}$ above the horizontal and 4.94 s later it lands 82.3 m from the base of the building. How tall is the building?

A train rounds a curve, with a radius of 300 m, at constant speed. A pendulum, suspended from the ceiling of the train, swings out at an angle of $15.0^{\circ}$ from the vertical as the train rounds the curve. Find the speed of the train.

A pitcher throws a baseball toward home plate. The 143-g ball is traveling horizontally at a speed of $32.2 \, \text{m/s}$ when the hitter's bat strikes the ball and the ball rises straight up to a height of 51.6 m directly above the point that the bat made contact with the ball. If the bat was in contact with the ball for 0.633 ms, find the magnitude of the average force applied to the ball.
A 6.00-m-long ladder is leaning against a frictionless wall as shown. The ladder is uniform and has a mass of 15.0 kg.

A 0.455 kg block is attached to a horizontal spring with a force constant of $44.0 \, \text{N/m}$. The block is pulled 12.5 cm from the equilibrium position and released from rest. Find the speed of the block when it is 8.00 cm from the equilibrium position.

A cabin in Sun Valley has a glass window that measures 2.50 m by 1.50 m and it is 1.25 cm thick. The temperature of the outer surface of the glass is $-18.0^{\circ}C$ and the temperature on the inner surface is $18.0^{\circ}C$. Find the rate of heat flow through the glass window. ($k_{\text{glass}}=0.963 \, \text{J/(s} \cdot \text{m} \cdot \text{C}^{\circ}\text{)}$)
Find the equivalent resistance for the circuit shown.

A 2.00-g particle with a charge of 922 nC is attached to the end of a 60.0 cm-long, insulating string. The electric field is uniform and horizontal. The particle is in equilibrium when the angle between the string and the vertical is $32.4^{\circ}$. Find the magnitude of the electric field. Ignore the mass of the string.

A 0.259-T magnetic field is directed vertically upward. An electron traveling horizontally at a speed of $5.18 \times 10^{6} \, \text{m/s}$ enters the field. Find the magnitude of the magnetic force on the electron.
A light ray in air enters a liquid at an angle of incidence of $40.0^{\circ}$. The ray is refracted at an angle of $26.4^{\circ}$. Find the speed of light in the liquid.
Find the distance that an object must be placed from a converging lens, with a focal length of 25.0 cm, to produce a real image that is five times as large as the object.

The physics class at Borger HS did a photoelectric experiment. A stopping potential of 0.158 V was measured when green light with a wavelength of 540 nm was incident on a caesium target. Find the stopping potential when ultraviolet light with a wavelength 300 nm is incident on the same caesium target.
$\pi^{-} + p \rightarrow p + e^{-} + \underline{\text{__________}}$ In the reaction above, which of the following particles will make the reaction possible?
Which of the following describes the behavior of light as a particle?

A long straight wire, cylindrical in shape, carries a 24.0 A current of uniform current density. The radius of the wire is 3.00 mm. Find the magnitude of the magnetic field at a point 2.00 mm from the central axis of the wire, far from the ends of the wire.
Apollo 17 was the last manned mission to the Moon. How many human beings have set foot on the surface of the Moon?
In this equation, $v$ is given in [meters/second], $m$ is in [kilograms], and $t$ is in [seconds]. What are the units of $Z$? __________ $Z = \frac{mv}{t}$

A horse-drawn chariot is moving at 12.0 m/s when it encounters a rough stretch of road. The driver directs the horses to slow down, reducing the speed of the chariot to 5.00 m/s. While it is slowing down, the chariot travels a distance of 45.0 m. What is the acceleration of the chariot while it is slowing down?

A heavy crate with a mass of 190.0 kg is hanging from a cable attached to the ceiling. The crate is pulled to one side by a factory worker using a horizontal chain (as shown). If the cable makes an angle of $60.0^{\circ}$ with the horizontal, what is the force, $F$, exerted by the factory worker?
You throw a 145 g baseball directly upward so that it just reaches a tree branch that is 12.5 m above the ground. At what speed did you throw the baseball?

A massive grindstone is used to sharpen tools in a workshop. The grindstone is a solid uniform disk with a mass of 45.0 kg and a radius of 0.350 m. If the grindstone starts from rest, how much work must be done on the stone to get it up to an angular speed of 600.0 rpm?

You are at a playground with a small child. The child is swinging on a swing set with chains that are 2.20 m long. You have the task of pushing the child each time that she swings back to you. How many times per minute must you push the swinging child?
A plastic toy floats in methanol (a liquid with a density of $792 \, \text{kg/m}^3$). Approximately 20% of the toy shows above the surface of the liquid. What is the density of the plastic toy?

You are testing a potato slice for electrical conductivity. The potato slice is 14.5 cm long and has a square cross section that is 0.40 cm by 0.40 cm. When connected to a 12.0 V power supply, the current flowing through the potato slice is $127 \, \mu\text{A}$. What is the resistivity of the potato slice?

A single isolated point charge, $Q = +4.50 \, \text{mC}$, is held at a fixed location. A second point charge, $q = +1.50 \, \mu\text{C}$, is initially held at a distance of 1.25 m from the fixed charge. The second charge is then released. What is the kinetic energy of the second charge when it is 4.50 m from the fixed charge?
How can you remove the permanent magnetization of a ferromagnetic material?
options: Heat the material to a high temperature
Cool the material to a very low temperature
Flow a DC current through the material
Expose the material to electromagnetic waves

question: A beam of polarized light with an intensity of $600.0 \, \text{W/m}^2$ is directed at a second polarizer. If the axis of the second polarizer is at an angle of $40.0^{\circ}$ with respect to the polarization of the incoming beam, what is the intensity of the light that passes through the second polarizer?

A candle is placed 27.0 cm in front of a concave mirror with a radius of curvature of 80.0 cm. What is the height of the image of the candle as seen in the mirror? The height of the candle is 3.50 cm.
A hydrogen atom undergoes a transition from the $n = 7$ state to the $n = 3$ state. What is the wavelength of the photon emitted during this transition?

The first evidence for the bottom-quark was found at Fermilab in 1977 with the discovery of the $\Upsilon$-particle (Upsilon-particle). The lifetime of the $\Upsilon$-particle is about $1.2 \times 10^{-20}$ seconds, and its decay produces two photons: $\Upsilon \rightarrow \gamma + \gamma$. What fundamental force is primarily responsible for the decay of the $\Upsilon$-particle?
Shown below is the position-versus-time graph for a toy train moving in one dimension. Which statement best describes the motion of the train?
options: Moving backward at a constant speed
Moving forward at a constant speed
Moving at a constant speed, but it is impossible to tell which direction

question: The graph shows the electric field between two parallel plates as a function of the voltage across the plates. What is the distance between the two parallel plates?

Ionized hydrogen regions in interstellar space (called H-II regions) are indicative of active star formation. Heated by nearby hot stars, the hydrogen in these regions generally glows what visible color?

For the following calculation, select the answer that has the correct number of significant figures.

$\frac{1.98 \times 10^3 - 2.002 \times 10^3}{19.3}$

An evil squirrel throws a pecan directly downward with an initial velocity of 5.25 m/s. The pecan is thrown from a point that is 2.29 m directly above your head. How fast is the pecan moving when it hits the top of your head?

In the system shown, the pulley is a uniform disk with a mass of 2.50 kg and a radius of 20.0 cm. The rope is massless and as it unwinds, it does not slip on the pulley. Once released, what is the linear acceleration of the mass?

A 300.0 g red block is moving to the right at 5.20 m/s. It strikes a 420.0 g green block that is initially at rest. After the collision, the red block is moving to the left at 0.850 m/s. What is the velocity of the green block after the collision?

A uniform beam is 5.00 m long and has a mass of 45.0 kg. It is supported by a hinge at one end and a cable at the other end as shown. If the cable makes an angle of $35.0^\circ$ with the beam, what is the tension in the cable?

A strong wind blows across some hollow reeds that stick up from the water at the edge of a nearby lake. As the wind blows, the reeds make a sound, resonating at a frequency of about 280.0 Hz. If we assume that this sound is the fundamental frequency, and if we treat the reeds as open-closed pipes, then what is the height (above the water) of the average reed? Assume the air temperature is $20^\circ\text{C}$.

A steel bridge is made of 12.5 m long sections. In the summer, at a temperature of $45^\circ\text{C}$, the sections are placed so that the gap between them is 0.50 cm. What is the width of the gap during the winter, when the temperature is $-10^\circ\text{C}$? The coefficient of linear expansion for steel is $1.0 \times 10^{-5} \, ^\circ\text{C}^{-1}$.
For the circuit shown, what is the current flowing through the $1800 \, \Omega$ resistor?

A $+0.50 \, \mu\text{C}$ charge is at the origin. A $+1.20 \, \mu\text{C}$ charge is at $x = -1.00 \, \text{cm}$, and a $-1.55 \, \mu\text{C}$ charge is at $x = +2.00 \, \text{cm}$. What is the magnitude of the net electrostatic force on the charge at the origin?

A railgun is constructed as shown, with rails that are 3.50 m long and separated by 25.0 cm. The rails are placed in a magnetic field of 0.850 T. A 150.0 g projectile is set on the rails, and a current of 60.0 A is sent through the projectile. Assuming that it starts from rest, what is the speed of the projectile when it reaches the end of the rails?

Unpolarized light travelling in air is incident upon a polished resin tabletop. Using a polarizer, you determine that the light reflected from the tabletop is perfectly polarized when the angle of reflection is $61.0^\circ$. What is the index of refraction of the resin tabletop?

You use a thin converging lens to look at a coin. The letters on the coin are 3.00 mm tall, but through the lens they appear to be 7.00 mm tall. If the coin is 4.00 cm from the lens, what is the focal length of the lens?
A subatomic particle has a lifetime of $1.50 \times 10^{-15} \, \text{s}$. What is the minimum uncertainty in the energy of the particle?

While living on Mt. Olympus, you give Zeus a 200.0 g sphere of pure Uranium-235 as a birthday gift. Zeus puts the sphere on a bookshelf and forgets about it. After 280 million years, Zeus is cleaning and finds the sphere. How much mass of Uranium-235 remains in the sphere? Note: the half-life of U-235 is 700 million years.
The graph shows the period-squared ($T^2$) of a pendulum versus the length of the pendulum ($\ell$). Based on this graph, what is the acceleration due to gravity in this location?
The illustration below represents the equipotential surfaces in a small region of space. At which of the labeled points is the electric field greatest in strength?
You discover a star that has an absolute luminosity that is about twice that of the Sun, but a temperature that is about half that of the Sun. Where on an HR-diagram would this star be located?

A steel bar that has dimensions 14.0 in by 20.0 in by 5.00 in is stretched into a wire. The wire has a circular cross section with a diameter of 3.30 mm. What is the length of the wire that can be made from this bar of steel?

An arrow is fired at an angle of 32.5° above the horizontal. The arrow strikes a target located 230.0 m downrange from where it was fired. The range is level, so the arrow strikes the target at the same height from which it was fired. What was the initial speed of the arrow? Ignore air resistance.

A mass of 12.5 kg hangs from a wire that goes up and over a massless/frictionless pulley and attaches to a 6.80 kg box sitting on a table (as shown). The coefficient of friction between the 6.80 kg box and the table is 0.250. What is the acceleration of the box?

A 5.50 g acorn falls from a tree branch located 13.8 m above the ground. As the acorn falls, air resistance exerts a constant upward force of $12.0 \times 10^{-3} \text{ N}$ on it. What is the speed of the acorn when it reaches the ground?

A tree branch has a swing attached to it and there is a 60.0 kg child sitting at rest on the swing. The tree branch is horizontal, and the swing is attached at a point 1.35 m from the trunk of the tree. A second rope, tied at the same point on the branch, goes up at an angle of 30° and attaches to the trunk. The branch has a mass of 25.0 kg and its center of mass is located 0.500 m from the trunk. What is the vertical force exerted on the trunk by the branch?

You set up a standing wave by oscillating a rope at a frequency of 2.50 Hz. The wave you set up is shown below. The tension in the rope is 18.0 N and the length of the rope is 4.00 m. What is the total mass of the rope?

A dark world orbits the remnants of a dead star. The average temperature of the dark world is $-160.0^{\circ}\text{C}$. The dark world is a sphere with a radius of $2.5 \times 10^{5} \text{ m}$ and a surface emissivity of 0.45. How much energy is radiated into space each day from this cooling world?
For this capacitor circuit, what is the voltage difference between points A and B?

Determine the work needed to move a $+12.0\mu\text{C}$ charge from the point $(-4.0, 0) \text{ cm}$ to the origin $(0, 0)$, as indicated. There is a fixed charge of $+18.0\mu\text{C}$ located at the point $(5.0, 3.0) \text{ cm}$.

A coil containing 200 turns of wire is placed in a magnetic field such that the field is perpendicular to the face of the coil (as shown). The radius of the coil is 12.0 cm and the magnetic field strength is 6000 Gauss. If the magnetic field strength decreases to 2500 Gauss in a time of 150.0 ms, what is the voltage induced in the coil?

The beam from a 200 W flashlight is directed onto a wall, producing a circular spot of light with a diameter of 32.0 cm. What is the magnitude of the electric field at the wall due to the light's electromagnetic wave?

A feather is placed 80.0 cm in front of a double-lens telescope (as shown). The first lens has a focal length of $+50.0 \text{ cm}$ and the second lens has a focal length of $+18.0 \text{ cm}$. The lenses are separated by 34.0 cm. Where is the final image of the feather located?
options: 15.2 cm right of the second lens
22.0 cm right of the second lens
30.8 cm right of the second lens
33.3 cm right of the second lens
99.3 cm right of the second lens

question: A certain atomic species has an energy level diagram as shown below. Knowing that allowed atomic transitions require $\Delta l = \pm 1$, which of the following photon wavelengths would you not expect to see emitted from a group of these atoms?
options: all of these would be expected.

question: The following nuclear reaction involves the absorption of a low-speed neutron by a Uranium-235 nucleus: ${}_{0}^{1}\text{n} + {}_{92}^{235}\text{U} \rightarrow {}_{37}^{94}\text{Rb} + {}_{55}^{140}\text{Cs} + x{}_{0}^{1}\text{n}$. What is the value of $x$?

Using the setup shown, you measure the tension in a string attached to a plastic cylinder as you lower the cylinder into a beaker of pure water. You plot the tension in the string as a function of the percent volume of the cylinder that is submerged in the water. From this plot, determine the density of the plastic cylinder.

You collect the gamma ray spectrum from an unknown radioactive sample. You use Cobalt-60, which produces peaks at 1.17 MeV and 1.33 MeV, for calibration. Based on this data and the table of known gamma ray energies below, determine the composition of the unknown sample.
During the early universe (immediately following the Big Bang), nucleosynthesis produced nuclei of helium and lithium. Roughly how long did this period of nucleosynthesis last?

On average, in the Chihuahuan desert, geologic weathering processes remove one cubic inch of rock every day from an exposed outcrop. If the average density of the rock is $2400 \, \text{kg/m}^3$, what mass of rock is eroded from the outcrop in a typical year (365.25 days)?

A boat starts from rest and travels through a stray current in the water. The current provides a constant acceleration of $1.50 \, \text{m/s}^2$ in the Northward direction. If the boat's motor is providing a constant velocity of $10.5 \, \text{m/s}$ in the Eastward direction, what is the speed of the boat after it has travelled $13.0 \, \text{m}$ Eastward?
A mass of $150.0 \, \text{kg}$ is held aloft by several ropes as shown. Find the tension, $T_1$, in the rightmost rope.

A crate of coffee is placed at the top of an inclined plane, at a point that is $1.25 \, \text{m}$ above the floor. The plane is inclined at an angle of $40.0^{\circ}$ above the horizontal; and the total length of the inclined plane is $1.94 \, \text{m}$ (as shown). The coefficient of friction between the plane and the crate is $0.190$. After it is released, the crate of coffee slides down the incline. What is the speed of the crate when it reaches the floor?

James Bond and Hugo Drax are fighting over a wheel valve of a space station. The wheel valve has a radius of $25.0 \, \text{cm}$ and a rotational inertia of $6.0 \, \text{kg} \cdot \text{m}^2$. James Bond is pulling on the outer edge of the wheel with a force of $95.0 \, \text{N}$, while Hugo Drax is pulling on one of the spokes, at a distance of $15.0 \, \text{cm}$ from the center, with a force of $75.0 \, \text{N}$. Both are pulling in such a way that their forces are perpendicular to the spoke. If the wheel is initially at rest, how long does it take for the wheel to make two full revolutions?

You are driving along the highway at a speed of $28.0 \, \text{m/s}$. A state trooper is travelling in the opposite direction, moving towards you at an unknown speed. The siren on the trooper's car emits a frequency of $450.0 \, \text{Hz}$ when stationary, but on the highway, you hear the trooper's siren at a frequency of $610.0 \, \text{Hz}$. If the air temperature is $20.0^{\circ}\text{C}$ then how fast is the trooper driving?

A piston with a diameter of $34.0 \, \text{cm}$ is pushed down by a force of $9000.0 \, \text{N}$ into a cylinder full of water. The cylinder of water is $4.25 \, \text{m}$ deep and has a small pipe connected at the bottom. The pipe has a diameter of $1.50 \, \text{cm}$ and empties into an open pond. What is the speed of the water as it sprays out of the open end of the pipe?
For the circuit shown, find the current flowing through the $1200 \, \Omega$ resistor.

Three point charges are placed in the $x$-$y$ plane: $Q_1 = +4.00 \, \mu\text{C}$ is at $(3.00, 0) \, \text{cm}$, $Q_2 = -6.00 \, \mu\text{C}$ is at $(0, 4.00) \, \text{cm}$, and $Q_3 = +2.00 \, \mu\text{C}$ is at $(3.00, 4.00) \, \text{cm}$. What is the magnitude of the total electric field at the origin $(0,0)$?

A rectangular loop of wire ($15.0 \, \text{cm}$ by $20.0 \, \text{cm}$) has a resistance of $22.5 \, \Omega$. The loop is placed in a uniform magnetic field of $9000 \, \text{G}$ such that the plane of the loop is perpendicular to the magnetic field. The loop is then rotated so that the plane of the loop is at a $45.0^{\circ}$ angle to the magnetic field. If this rotation takes $0.350 \, \text{s}$, what is the average current induced in the loop during the rotation?
For this AC circuit, determine the magnitude of the current (rms) flowing through the $60.0 \, \Omega$ resistor.

An air wedge is formed between two flat plates of glass (as shown). The angle between the plates is $0.030^{\circ}$. A laser with a wavelength of $575.0 \, \text{nm}$ is directed onto the glass plates from above. How far apart, horizontally, are the dark fringes resulting from thin film interference in the air wedge?
In the Bohr model of the atom, which of the following was a major failing that was later corrected by the Schrödinger model?
options: The Bohr model required electrons to move in circular orbits, which was known to be impossible.
The energy levels in a Bohr atom get closer together as you move farther out from the nucleus instead of getting farther apart.
The Bohr model could still not explain the atomic emission line spectrum for any element.
Some energy levels in the Bohr model contained more electrons than other energy levels did.
In Bohr's "solar system" model atoms were flat disks, but atoms were known to be spherical.

question: What is the binding energy per nucleon of Ytterbium-164 ($^{164}_{70}\text{Yb}$)? The mass of a nucleus of this isotope of Ytterbium is $163.934489 \, \text{amu}$.

A ball with a mass of $210.0 \, \text{g}$ is placed on a vertical spring. The ball is pressed down, compressing the spring, and then released. The maximum height (measured from the release point) to which the ball launches is measured for different spring compression amounts. The data is graphed below. From this data, estimate the spring constant of the spring.

A compass is placed a short distance directly above a long, straight, horizontal wire. The wire carries a DC current in the North-South direction. The deflection of the compass needle from North is measured for different currents in the wire. The data is graphed below. From this data, estimate the current flowing in the wire when the compass is $4.0 \, \text{cm}$ above the wire and the needle deflects $54^{\circ}$. Assume the horizontal component of the Earth's magnetic field is $5.0 \times 10^{-5} \, \text{T}$.

The equation relating apparent magnitude, absolute magnitude, and distance for a star is written as: $d = (10 \text{pc}) \times 10^{(m-M)/5}$. Which of the following is the correct definition for absolute magnitude?
options: The brightness of a star as seen from Earth.
The brightness of a star as if it were 10 parsecs away.
The total energy output of a star per second.
The apparent brightness of a star if its distance were unknown.
The temperature of the star's photosphere.

question: A golf ball is hit from the top of a hill, 12.0 m above the green. The initial speed of the ball is 25.1 m/s. The ball flies along the trajectory shown, and without bouncing, plops directly into the hole. The hole is 74.0 m (horizontally) from where the ball was hit. At which of these angles (relative to the horizontal) could the ball have been initially struck?

A 10.0 kg box of tea is placed on a ramp that is inclined at an angle of 50.0^{\circ} relative to the horizontal. A rope that is attached to the box of tea passes over a frictionless pulley to a 4.00 kg hanging mass. The coefficient of friction between the box of tea and the ramp is 0.24. Once released, what is the acceleration of the box of tea?
A toy slides horizontally across a surface. If the toy starts from rest, how can you determine the speed of the toy from the work done on it by a varying force?
options: Find the slope of the Force-Distance graph.
Divide the total force by the displacement.
Equate the final kinetic energy to the area under the Force-Distance curve.
Calculate the product of the average force and the time of travel.
Use the conservation of momentum equation.

question: A cylindrical tub is freely spinning on frictionless bearings at an angular speed of 30.0 rad/s. The tub can be treated as a cylindrical ring with a mass of 5.00 kg. The tub is slowly filled with sand, at a constant rate of 1.50 kg/s, until it contains 15.0 kg of sand. In the tub, the sand has the shape of a solid disk with the same radius as the tub. While sand was being added to the tub, what was the effective angular acceleration of the tub?

Two 100.0 W speakers are playing loud music at a dance competition. Assuming that the sound waves expand spherically from the speakers, and that the sound waves from the two speakers are not coherent, what is the sound intensity (in dB) at the listener's location?

1.60 mols of a diatomic ideal gas are taken through an engine cycle represented by the PV-diagram shown. How much heat energy must be added to the gas ($Q_{\text{in}}$) to go through one complete cycle?
What is the equivalent resistance of a circuit consisting of three resistors $R_{1}=100 \Omega$, $R_{2}=200 \Omega$, and $R_{3}=300 \Omega$ connected in parallel?
The voltage at (0.0, 0.0) produced by two fixed charges (+6.00 $\mu$C and +10.0 $\mu$C) placed at specific distances is calculated. What is the value of the voltage at this point?

A current of 49.66 A flows in a wire that has a radius of 2.50 mm. The current density in the wire is given by the function: $J(r)=5.0e^{-0.25r^{2}}$ where r is in mm. What is the magnetic field at a point inside the wire, 1.50 mm from the center of the wire?
Which of the following processes in a heat engine cycle involves heat energy flowing into the gas?

In this problem, we have a refracting surface followed by a lens. The object (the fly) is in amber, so $n_{1} = 1.55$; and the image will form in air, so $n_{2} = 1.00$. Where does the final image form?
__________ is a particle-antiparticle pair often involved in particle decay analyses.
In a Weak Force decay involving quark flavor change, which quark structure is a possible baryon with a charge of +1e?

Over several weeks, you plot the location of a moon that is orbiting an unknown planet. The orbit of the moon is perfectly circular, with a radius of $2.50 \times 10^{5}$ km. What is the approximate mass of the unknown planet?
__________ is the final stage in the life of a star, where the quantum fluctuations of space-time balance the weight of matter, according to loop quantum gravity.
The largest known volcano in our solar system is the Olympus Mons located on __________ . "It is a 27 km-high shield-type volcano, similar to but much larger than Hawaii's Mauna Loa."
8.54 Megameters = __________ Nanometers.
A helicopter is ascending vertically at a speed of 4.88 m/s. At a height of 100 m, a marble is released from a window in the helicopter. How much time does it take for the marble to reach the ground?
A pulling force of 20.0 N is applied as shown above. The coefficient of friction between the blocks and the surface is 0.285. Find the tension in the cord connecting the blocks.

On his vacation in Wyoming, Chuck decides to attempt a bungee jump off a bridge that spans the Yellowstone River. He jumps off the bridge with a bungee cord attached to his ankle. He falls 12.0 m before the cord begins to stretch. Chuck's mass is 200 kg and the elastic constant of the bungee cord is 175.0 N/m. How far below the bridge will Chuck fall before coming to rest?

A 25.0-g bullet is traveling at a speed of 600 m/s when it strikes and becomes embedded at the edge of a 4.20-kg cylinder which has a 60.0 cm radius. The cylinder, which was initially at rest, begins to rotate about its axis. Find the angular speed of the cylinder after the collision.
A piano string is 1.08-m-long and it has a mass of 12.0 g. The string is under 880 N of tension. Find the frequency of the third harmonic.

A 20.0-m length of copper pipe is initially at a temperature of 18.0 C when it was taken outside and placed in the sun on a hot day. If the temperature of the copper rod reached 43.0 C, by how much did the length expand? (a=17x10^-6)
Find the maximum charge the capacitor will acquire when the switch is closed.

An equilateral triangle, AABC, has a 22.2 uC charged particle at each vertex. If each side of the triangle is 15.0 cm long, what is the magnitude of the force exerted on the charged particle located at A by the particles located at B and C?

A horizontal wire carries a 60-A current to the right. A second wire, parallel to the top wire, is 6.2 mm below the first wire and carries a current of 20 A to the right. Both wires are 4.8 m long. If the force of attraction between the wires is just enough to keep the lower wire from falling due to gravity, what is the mass of the lower wire?

A square coil of wire lies in the plane of the page. It contains 60 loops and each side of the square is 3.0 cm long. It is positioned perpendicular to a uniform 0.88-T magnetic field coming out of the page. It is uniformly pulled from the field, moving perpendicular to the field. At t=0, the right edge of the coil is at the edge of the field. It takes 0.025 s for the entire coil to exit the field. If the resistance of the coil is 25 , what is the average induced current?
A candle is placed 42 cm from a convex mirror. The radius of curvature of the mirror is 56 cm. Find the magnification of the image.

The Lubbock Avalanche Journal reported that the starship Red Raider just returned from a ten-year journey while traveling at a speed of 0.911c. If the report meant ten years of Earth time, how much time elapsed on the starship?

The following reaction is not possible because at least one of the conservation laws is violated. Determine which conservation laws are not violated. K+ + n -> E+ + pi0 + gamma I. Charge II. Baryon Number III. Strangeness IV. Lepton Number

The physics class at La Feria is doing a lab in which a ball that is attached to a string revolves in a nearly horizontal circle of radius 1.4 m. The tension in the cord is measured at various velocities. The data was plotted and the LSRL was sketched. Use the graph above to calculate the mass of the ball, within +1.0%.

The temperature inside Darren's cabin in the Colorado mountains loses heat through a rectangular glass window that measures 2.0 m by 4.0 m. (Great view, large heat loss) The glass window is 2.5 cm thick. He kept the inside temperature at a constant 20 C while he was there during a 5-day vacation last April. The rate of heat flow through the window was greater on colder days because the difference between the inside temperature and outside temperature was greater. The data for each day was plotted and the LSRL was sketched. From the graph above, calculate the thermal conductivity of glass, within +1.0%.
The first discoveries of exoplanets included many hot __________ which often orbit solar-type stars in "highly eccentric orbits that get closer to their parent stars than Mercury is to our own Sun."
The mass of the Earth is estimated to be $5.9722 \times 10^{24}$ kg or __________ Tg
A drone was flying horizontally at a speed of $24.0 \text{ m/s}$ and at a height of $48.0 \text{ m}$ above the ground. It released a water balloon when it was directly __________.

If the coefficient of kinetic friction between the surface of the table and the $2.40 \text{ kg}$ wooden block is $0.335$, find the acceleration of the $2.40 \text{ kg}$ wooden block when both blocks are released. Assume the pully is massless and frictionless.

A $45.2 \text{ g}$ bullet is fired vertically into a $1.56 \text{ kg}$ wooden block that is at rest on a thin sheet. If the bullet is traveling at a speed of $326 \text{ m/s}$ and then becomes embedded in the block, how high above the sheet will the block/bullet rise?
A mass of $2.45 \text{ kg}$ is attached to a spring with a constant $k = 185 \text{ N/m}$. What is the period of oscillation for this system?
A simple pendulum has a length of $1.50 \text{ m}$. What is the period of this pendulum on a planet where $g = 8.80 \text{ m/s}^{2}$?
A $5.00 \text{ kg}$ block is sliding down an incline of $25.0^{\circ}$ with a coefficient of kinetic friction of $0.200$. What is the acceleration of the block?

A $125 \text{ V}$ source is connected to a circuit with a $15.0 \text{ } \Omega$ resistor, a $25.0 \text{ } \Omega$ inductor, and a $35.0 \text{ } \Omega$ capacitor in series at a frequency of $60.0 \text{ Hz}$. What is the impedance of the circuit?
A $10.0 \text{ cm}$ tall object is placed $20.0 \text{ cm}$ in front of a converging lens with a focal length of $15.0 \text{ cm}$. What is the image distance?
What is the speed of light in a material with an index of refraction of $1.55$?

$E = 3.22 \times 10^{4} \text{ N/C}$, between two charged parallel plates. The separation of the plates is $1.08 \text{ cm}$. An electron is placed near the negative plate and released. It accelerates from rest and passes through a tiny hole in the positive plate. Find the speed of the electron as it leaves the hole.
What is the energy of a photon with a wavelength of $550 \text{ nm}$?
What is the magnetic field $5.00 \text{ cm}$ from a long, straight wire carrying a current of $10.0 \text{ A}$?
A $2.00 \text{ cm}$ diameter loop with $50$ turns is placed in a magnetic field that changes from $0.200 \text{ T}$ to $0.800 \text{ T}$ in $0.500 \text{ s}$. What is the induced electromotive force?

The physics class at Martin is doing a lab in which a vertical spring has various masses hung from it. The mass is pulled down and oscillates vertically. The mass and the period are recorded for five different trials. The data is plotted and the LSRL is graphed. Find the value of the spring constant within $\pm1.0\%$.

The physics class at Reagan is doing a lab in which the resistance is measured for various lengths of wire. All lengths used platinum wire with a cross sectional area of $5.3 \times 10^{-6} \text{ m}^{2}$. The data was plotted and the LSRL was graphed. Find the resistivity of platinum within $\pm1\%$.

Assume that an astronomer found a new star and he estimated the distance from the Sun to the newly discovered star by measuring the parallax of the star. If the parallax of the star is 1.00 arcsec, what is the estimated distance from the Sun to the star?
The radius of Earth is 6378 km at the equator and 6357 km at either pole. The difference in these two values is __________ nm.
A ball is thrown horizontally at 25.9 m/s from the roof of the Borger Civic Center. If the ball lands 52.6 m from the base of the building, how high above the ground was the ball released?
A 12.0-kg box is released at the top of a 30.0$^{\circ}$ incline and accelerates down the incline at 0.885 m/s$^{2}$. Find the coefficient of kinetic friction between the box and the incline.
A 2,000-kg car climbs a 5.00$^{\circ}$ hill at a steady 90.0 km/h. Due to rolling and air friction, there is a constant retarding force of 900 N as the car climbs the hill. Find the power required.

A uniform 2.00-kg rod is 50.0 cm-long and it is free to pivot about a pin attached to the wall. The rod is held horizontally and released. Find the linear acceleration of the tip of the rod at the moment the rod is released. ($I=\frac{1}{3}ML^{2}$)
Two successive harmonics of a vibrating string are 340 Hz and 408 Hz. Find the frequency of the first overtone.

An air bubble at the bottom of a lake 33.3 m deep has a volume of 0.822 cm$^{3}$. The temperature at the bottom of the lake is 8.47$^{\circ}$C and at the top 20.4$^{\circ}$C. Find the volume of the bubble just as it reaches the surface.
The voltmeter reads __________ volts.
When two charged particles are placed 5.00 m apart, they exert a force of 0.0021576 N on each other. How much force will they exert on each other if they are placed 25.0 m apart?

A long straight cylindrical wire conductor is 20.0 m long and carries a current of 12.0 A, which is uniformly distributed across its cross section. If the diameter of the wire is 88.8 mm, find the magnetic field at a point that is a radial distance of 11.1 mm from the central axis of the wire.

A 56.6 cm-long-conductor is pulled across the page to the right at a speed of 12.5 m/s. There is a 1.33-T magnetic field that is oriented out of the page and perpendicular to the path of the conductor. Find the induced emf.
A 2.00-in-tall candle is placed 18 cm from a concave mirror with a radius of curvature of 42 cm. Find the magnification of the image.
A distant star emits light whose peak intensity is in the ultraviolet spectrum with a wavelength of 121 nm. Find the surface temperature of the star.
Complete the nuclear reaction. $^{254}_{98}Cf\rightarrow^{118}_{46}Pd+\_+4^{1}_{0}n$

The physics class at Sabine is doing a lab in which a large, stiff, Hooke's Law spring is stretched various amounts and the work done on the spring is calculated. Use the plot of their data to find the spring constant.

The physics class at Hirschi is doing a lab in which the capacitance of a parallel plate capacitor is calculated with various dielectrics inserted between the plates. The area of each plate is 2.44 m$^{2}$. Use the plot of their data to find the plate separation.
The rotation of the planet __________ is clockwise, which causes the sun to rise in the west. Scientists are still puzzled by this backward rotation.

A standard football field is 360 ft long and 160 ft wide. The Haliade-X 12 MW wind turbine has three 107-m-long blades. These form a rotor with a diameter of 220 m. The area swept by the blades is equivalent to the area of __________ football fields. ($1 \text{ in} = 2.54 \text{ cm}$)
The Defiance was a 46-gun galleon of the English Royal Navy launched in 1590. If the guns had a range of 300 m, with what speed did the shot leave the barrels of the guns?

The Drum Jay. In the last chapter of his book, Seven Brief Lessons on Physics, Carlo Rovelli discusses and states "This, I believe, is one of the most interesting frontiers of science, where major progress is about to be made." __________

A batting practice machine delivers a 141-g baseball that travels horizontally at a speed of 42.2 m/s. Tye swings his bat horizontally, striking the ball. The ball leaves the bat at a speed of 48.8 m/s at an angle of 33.3$^{\circ}$ above the horizontal. The bat is in contact with the ball for 1.11 ms. Find the impulse that acts on the ball while it is in contact with the bat.

A 74.3-kg boy and a 58.7-kg girl are on a merry-go-round with a radius of 2.10 m. If the boy and girl hold on to the edge of the merry-go-round, which is rotating at 12.0 rpm, what is the total centripetal force experienced by the boy and girl?
A 6.00 kg block is attached to a spring with a constant of 777 N/m. The system is set into oscillation with an amplitude of 0.350 m. Find the maximum velocity of the block.

Water is traveling through a pipe with an inner diameter of 4.00 cm in the basement of a house at a speed of 88.6 cm/s and at a pressure of 210 kPa. The pipe rises 10.0 m to the third floor of the house where the inner diameter of the pipe is reduced to 2.80 cm. Find the water pressure at the third floor.
A 72 $\Omega$ resistor is connected in series with a 12 V battery. If an ammeter is added to the circuit and reads 0.462 A, what is the internal resistance of the battery?

An alpha particle, $m = 6.644 \times 10^{-27}$ kg, is orbiting a charged sphere just above the surface at a speed of $4.66 \times 10^{5}$ m/s. The sphere has a radius of 2.00 cm. Find the charge on the sphere.

The cross section of a hollow conducting cylinder has an inner radius of 3.00 cm and an outer radius of 6.00 cm. The cylinder carries a current into the page. The current density is given by $J = __________$. Find the magnitude of the magnetic field at a point 5.00 cm from the central axis of the cylinder.
Find the maximum current amplitude. There are two angular frequencies which will produce a current amplitude that is exactly half of this maximum value. Find the larger of the two frequencies.
A candle is placed 24 cm from a converging lens with a focal length __________.
X-rays with a wavelength of 48 pm are incident on a crystal. The first-order diffraction maximum is observed at an angle of 22.0$^{\circ}$. What is the spacing between the crystal planes?
Complete the beta decay equation: $^{210}_{82}\text{Pb} \rightarrow __________ + \text{e}^{-} + \bar{\nu}_{e}$

The Starship Enterprise recently visited a system of planets that orbit the star Troy III. Use the graph above, which has the LSRL and five data points, to find the mass of Troy III within 1.00% of the true value.

The physics class at Sundown is doing a lab with their new Bubble Chamber. In the lab, ions with a charge of $__________$, but of unknown mass, travel horizontally and enter the chamber where there is a 120 mT vertical magnetic field. The velocity of the ion for each trial is dependent on the voltage of the source used to accelerate the ion to the speed with which it enters the field. For each trial, the voltage is recorded and the radius of the circular path of the ion in the field is carefully measured. Assume the source produced ions of the same mass for each of the five trials. Use the graph above, which has the LSRL and five data points, to determine the mass of the ions produced by the source for this lab within 1.00% of the true value.
